title: Write a Production-Grade Script
description:
## Fail Fast, Fail Loud

Production scripts must not silently continue after an error or on an unset variable.

**Requirements:** create an executable script at **`/root/deploy.sh`** that:
1. enables strict mode — **`set -euo pipefail`**
2. requires **one argument** (an environment name); if it is missing, print a **usage message to stderr** and exit **non-zero**
3. when given an argument `X`, prints exactly **`Deploying to X`**

> 💡 Guard the argument with `[ $# -lt 1 ]` *before* using `$1` (otherwise `set -u` will error). Don't forget `chmod +x`.

hints.title: Strict-mode skeleton
hints.body: Enable errexit/nounset/pipefail, validate arg count, write usage to stderr (>&2) and exit 1, then do the work.
hints.command:
printf '#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
  echo "usage: deploy.sh <env>" >&2
  exit 1
fi
echo "Deploying to $1"
' > /root/deploy.sh && chmod +x /root/deploy.sh

validation.description: Checks the script is executable, uses strict mode, errors on missing arg, and works with one.

commands.description: Script is executable and enables strict mode
commands.command: test -x /root/deploy.sh && grep -qE 'set -euo pipefail|set -o errexit' /root/deploy.sh && echo yes

commands.description: Missing argument prints a usage message to stderr
With an argument it prints the deploy line
